rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE J. MALINOWSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/01/2022. Examiner does not have the required courtesy copy of the amended petition. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Examiner does not an updated proposed order that matches what is requested in the amended petition. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANITA RADHIKA LOREK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real Property And For Order Directing Its Transfer To Decedents Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Direct notice to the decedent's two minor children was given on 12/09/2021, less than 30 days before this hearing. The petitioner alleges that no direct notice is required under Probate Code section 1460.1 because both children are under the age of 12. 2) The 2004 grant deed transferred title to the subject real property to the decedent and her parents as: "Anita Simon, a single woman as to an undivided 70% interest and Patrick Simon and Jayanthi Simon, Husband and Wife as Joint Tenants, as to an undivided 30% interest, all as joint tenancy". The petition alleges that the decedent intended to take title as a tenant in common to hold an undivided 70% interest with her parents holding an undivided 30% interest, with a joint tenancy only intended between the decedent's parents as to their 30% interest. 3) The petitioner filed a supplement to request reformation of the 2004 grant deed as additional relief sought to remove "all as joint tenancy" from the description of the grantees. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.
